questions.question: A piece of wood is a prism with a right-angled triangular cross-section of sides $9\text{ cm}, 12\text{ cm},$ and $15\text{ cm}$. If the length of the wood is $25\text{ cm}$, calculate its volume.
questions.explanation:
Area of triangle $= 0.5 \times 9 \times 12 = 54$. Volume $= 54 \times 25 = 1350\text{ cm}^3$.

**Common mistake:** The student might use the longest side ($15$) in the triangle area calculation by mistake.

questions.question: A rectangular box without a lid is built using small cubes. If the internal dimensions are $3 \times 3 \times 3$, how many small cubes are touching the sides or the bottom?
questions.explanation:
Total cubes in $3 \times 3 \times 3$ is 27. The only cubes NOT touching the sides/bottom are the $2 \times 1$ cubes in the center top layer? No, in a $3 \times 3 \times 3$, only the $1 \times 1 \times 2$ center cubes in an open box. Cubes touching $= 27 - 2 = 25$.

**Common mistake:** Miscounting the center core cubes that are not in contact with any 'walls' of the box.

questions.question: A solid metal sphere of radius $5\text{ cm}$ is melted down and recast into a solid cube. Find the length of a side of the cube correct to 2 decimal places.
questions.explanation:
Sphere Volume $= \frac{4}{3}\pi(5^3) \approx 523.598$. $s^3 = 523.598 \Rightarrow s = \sqrt{523.598} \approx 8.059$.

**Common mistake:** The student might assume the side of the cube is equal to the radius or diameter of the sphere.

questions.question: A piece of wood is a prism with a right-angled triangular cross-section of sides 9 cm, 12 cm and 15 cm. If the length is 25 cm, calculate its volume.
questions.explanation:
Volume = base area $\times$2$= 0.5 \times 9 \times 12 = 54$. Volume $= 54 \times 25 = 1350\text{ cm}^3$.

**Common mistake:** Using the hypotenuse (15) in the triangle area calculation ($0.5 \times 12 \times 15$).

questions.question: The ISS made 10,000 circular orbits travelling $4.259 \times 10^{8}$ km. Calculate the radius of the orbit to the nearest ten kilometres.
questions.explanation:
One orbit $= 42590 km$2$2C = 2\pi r \implies 42590 = 2\pi r \implies r \approx 6778.4$. Rounds to $6780$.

**Common mistake:** Rounding to the nearest whole number (6778) instead of the nearest ten (6780).
